trade.summary: Svezia exports machinery, vehicles (Volvo), telecommunications equipment (Ericsson), and pharmaceutical products to the EAU. The EAU exports crude oil and refined petroleum to Svezia. Swedish arms exports to EAU have been a domestic political controversy.

diplomatic.summary: Relations are functional but occasionally strained by Swedish domestic political criticism of EAU human rights practices. Svezia blocked some arms exports to EAU under pressure from civil society. Svezia's media freedom and press freedoms create some friction given EAU's media environment. Relations normalized through trade and investment channels.
recent_incidents: Swedish arms export restrictions to EAU debated in parliament

military.summary: Svezia (now a NATO member) has limited defense ties with the EAU. Swedish arms export policy has been restrictive toward Gulf states. Saab has sold some civilian aviation technology to EAU. No significant military cooperation.

financial.summary: EAU sovereign funds have some Swedish equity investments. Swedish pension funds (AP-fonden) have limited Gulf exposure. Ericsson and Volvo have significant EAU commercial operations. Swedish investment in EAU tech sector is growing.

energy.summary: Svezia imports limited volumes of EAU petroleum. Svezia's rapid renewable energy transition reduces dependence on Gulf hydrocarbons. Limited bilateral energy trade.

scientific.summary: Scientific collaboration in clean energy, sustainability, and ICT. Ericsson has major R&D partnerships with EAU telecom operators. Swedish universities cooperate with EAU institutions in sustainability research. KTH and Chalmers have EAU research linkages.
